title: Koyamacalia auriculata
taxonomy: Koyamacalia auriculata is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family Asteraceae. It is native to the southwestern part of Mexico. The classification of this plant places it in the tribe Senecioneae, which is a group of daisy-like plants. The plant is further classified as belonging to the genus Koyamacalia, which is a small group of flowering plants. Koyamacalia auriculata is closely related to other members of the Senecioneae tribe, sharing similar characteristics and growth habits.

intro: Koyamacalia auriculata, also known as Korean angelica, is a perennial plant native to East Asia, belonging to the family Asteraceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Koyamacalia auriculata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Koyamacalia auriculata include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
